title.original: Distribution and richness of Orthohantavirus reservoir hosts: Implications for zoonotic risk in Southern South America.

abstract.original: Orthohantaviruses represent a major zoonotic threat in Southern South America (SSA), where 13 native rodent species are recognized reservoir hosts of 15 different viral genotypes. The information on their geographic distribution remains scattered, limiting regional assessments of exposure risk. We compiled and curated occurrence records for the Orthohantavirus reservoir hosts in SSA and used ecological niche modeling to estimate their potential distributions. We integrated binary presence predictions to map reservoir and genotype richness as well as reservoir assemblages. The richness maps were combined with gridded human population data to estimate the number of people potentially exposed. Most Orthohantavirus reservoir species in SSA are distributed over eastern and northeastern Argentina, parts of Paraguay, Uruguay, and southern Brazil. Hotspots of reservoir richness were concentrated in the Humid Chaco, while the area of high genotype richness extends across the Chaco-Pampean plain. Exposure to a high diversity of pathogenic genotypes (≥7) was estimated to exceed 13 million people under the most precautionary scenario. This study provides the first integrated, region-wide assessment of Orthohantavirus reservoir distributions and richness in SSA, offering an updated ecological baseline to support surveillance, risk mapping, and prevention strategies.

sentences.text: Orthohantaviruses represent a major zoonotic threat in Southern South America (SSA), where 13 native rodent species are recognized reservoir hosts of 15 different viral genotypes.
The information on their geographic distribution remains scattered, limiting regional assessments of exposure risk.
We compiled and curated occurrence records for the Orthohantavirus reservoir hosts in SSA and used ecological niche modeling to estimate their potential distributions.
We integrated binary presence predictions to map reservoir and genotype richness as well as reservoir assemblages.
The richness maps were combined with gridded human population data to estimate the number of people potentially exposed.
Most Orthohantavirus reservoir species in SSA are distributed over eastern and northeastern Argentina, parts of Paraguay, Uruguay, and southern Brazil.
Hotspots of reservoir richness were concentrated in the Humid Chaco, while the area of high genotype richness extends across the Chaco-Pampean plain.
Exposure to a high diversity of pathogenic genotypes (≥7) was estimated to exceed 13 million people under the most precautionary scenario.

This study provides the first integrated, region-wide assessment of Orthohantavirus reservoir distributions and richness in SSA, offering an updated ecological baseline to support surveillance, risk mapping, and prevention strategies.

title.original: Hantavirus infections in a changing world: epidemiology, pathogenesis, and one health challenges.
title.translated_zh: 汉坦病毒感染在变化的世界中的流行病学、发病机制及一体化健康挑战

abstract.original: Hantavirus infections are zoonotic diseases of global relevance, characterised by significant morbidity and mortality and complex ecological dynamics. Transmitted primarily through rodent reservoirs, hantaviruses cause two major clinical syndromes: haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) and hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome (HCPS). Despite being historically confined to specific geographic regions, recent environmental changes, increased human animal interaction, and global mobility have contributed to evolving epidemiological patterns. This review summarises current knowledge on hantavirus transmission, global epidemiology, pathophysiological mechanisms, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches. Particular attention is given to the emerging global epidemiology of Seoul virus (SEOV), an urban-associated hantavirus linked to brown rats and pet rodents. In addition, it explores emerging challenges related to vaccine development, surveillance, and the integration of One Health strategies in the prevention and control of hantavirus infections. Strengthening multidisciplinary approaches and improving early detection remain critical to mitigating the global impact of these infections.
abstract.translated_zh: 汉坦病毒感染是一种具有全球重要性的动物源性疾病，表现为显著的发病率和死亡率，并具有复杂的生态动态。汉坦病毒主要通过啮齿动物宿主传播，导致两种主要临床综合征：出血热伴肾综合征（HFRS）和汉坦病毒心肺综合征（HCPS）。尽管历史上汉坦病毒感染局限于特定地理区域，但近期环境变化、人类与动物接触增加及全球流动性增强，促使流行病学模式发生演变。本文综述了汉坦病毒的传播、全球流行病学、病理生理机制、临床表现及治疗方法的最新知识。特别关注了与棕鼠和宠物啮齿动物相关的城市汉坦病毒——首尔病毒（SEOV）的新兴全球流行病学。此外，探讨了疫苗开发、监测以及在汉坦病毒感染预防和控制中整合一体化健康策略所面临的新挑战。加强多学科方法和改进早期检测对于减轻这些感染的全球影响至关重要。

sentences.text: Hantavirus infections are zoonotic diseases of global relevance, characterised by significant morbidity and mortality and complex ecological dynamics.
Transmitted primarily through rodent reservoirs, hantaviruses cause two major clinical syndromes: haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) and hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome (HCPS).

Despite being historically confined to specific geographic regions, recent environmental changes, increased human animal interaction, and global mobility have contributed to evolving epidemiological patterns.
This review summarises current knowledge on hantavirus transmission, global epidemiology, pathophysiological mechanisms, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches.
Particular attention is given to the emerging global epidemiology of Seoul virus (SEOV), an urban-associated hantavirus linked to brown rats and pet rodents.
In addition, it explores emerging challenges related to vaccine development, surveillance, and the integration of One Health strategies in the prevention and control of hantavirus infections.
Strengthening multidisciplinary approaches and improving early detection remain critical to mitigating the global impact of these infections.

display_summary.zh: 本文综述了汉坦病毒感染的传播途径、全球流行病学及临床表现，重点关注首尔病毒的城市传播及一体化健康策略在预防控制中的作用，强调多学科合作和早期检测的重要性。
display_summary.en: This review summarizes hantavirus transmission, global epidemiology, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches, highlighting the emerging urban spread of Seoul virus and the role of One Health strategies in prevention and control, emphasizing multidisciplinary collaboration and early detection.

ai_analysis.translated_title_zh: 汉坦病毒感染在变化的世界中的流行病学、发病机制及一体化健康挑战
ai_analysis.translated_abstract_zh: 汉坦病毒感染是一种具有全球重要性的动物源性疾病，表现为显著的发病率和死亡率，并具有复杂的生态动态。汉坦病毒主要通过啮齿动物宿主传播，导致两种主要临床综合征：出血热伴肾综合征（HFRS）和汉坦病毒心肺综合征（HCPS）。尽管历史上汉坦病毒感染局限于特定地理区域，但近期环境变化、人类与动物接触增加及全球流动性增强，促使流行病学模式发生演变。本文综述了汉坦病毒的传播、全球流行病学、病理生理机制、临床表现及治疗方法的最新知识。特别关注了与棕鼠和宠物啮齿动物相关的城市汉坦病毒——首尔病毒（SEOV）的新兴全球流行病学。此外，探讨了疫苗开发、监测以及在汉坦病毒感染预防和控制中整合一体化健康策略所面临的新挑战。加强多学科方法和改进早期检测对于减轻这些感染的全球影响至关重要。
ai_analysis.display_summary_zh: 本文综述了汉坦病毒感染的传播途径、全球流行病学及临床表现，重点关注首尔病毒的城市传播及一体化健康策略在预防控制中的作用，强调多学科合作和早期检测的重要性。
ai_analysis.display_summary_en: This review summarizes hantavirus transmission, global epidemiology, clinical manifestations, and therapeutic approaches, highlighting the emerging urban spread of Seoul virus and the role of One Health strategies in prevention and control, emphasizing multidisciplinary collaboration and early detection.
ai_analysis.one_sentence_takeaway: Environmental changes and increased human-animal interactions are driving evolving hantavirus epidemiology, necessitating integrated One Health approaches and improved early detection to mitigate global impact.
key_findings: Hantavirus infections cause significant morbidity and mortality with complex ecological dynamics (A1).
Transmission is primarily through rodent reservoirs causing HFRS and HCPS (A2).
Environmental changes, increased human-animal interaction, and global mobility have altered epidemiological patterns (A3).
Seoul virus (SEOV), associated with urban brown rats and pet rodents, shows emerging global epidemiology (A5).
Challenges exist in vaccine development, surveillance, and integrating One Health strategies for prevention and control (A6).
Multidisciplinary approaches and early detection are critical to mitigating global impact (A7).
ai_analysis.significance: The review highlights the evolving epidemiology of hantavirus infections driven by environmental and societal changes, emphasizing the need for integrated One Health approaches and improved surveillance to reduce global disease burden.
ai_analysis.limitations: The abstract does not specify study design, sample sizes, or detailed epidemiological data, limiting assessment of evidence strength and specific quantitative conclusions.
ai_analysis.evidence_strength: The evidence is based on a narrative review summarizing current knowledge without primary data or meta-analysis, limiting strength to expert synthesis.
uncertainties: Lack of detailed epidemiological data and quantitative metrics in the abstract.
No information on vaccine efficacy or specific surveillance outcomes.
Unclear geographic scope beyond mention of global and urban contexts.

title.original: The Orthohantavirus GN/GC Spikes: Molecular Determinants of Infection and Targets for Antiviral Immune Interventions.
title.translated_zh: 正汉坦病毒GN/GC刺突蛋白：感染的分子决定因素及抗病毒免疫干预的靶点

abstract.original: Orthohantaviruses are globally distributed, rodent-borne RNA viruses associated with severe and often lethal disease in humans. The documented person-to-person transmission of Andes virus, endemic in South America, underscores their pandemic potential, and yet no effective treatments are available, while vaccines exist only for Hantaan and Seoul viruses. Key targets for antiviral intervention are the tetrameric GN/GC spikes, which are displayed on the viral surface. Here we review current molecular and cellular insights into these spikes, focusing on their roles during the viral infection cycle, such as receptor-mediated cell entry, membrane fusion, viral assembly, and cell exit. We also examine recent advances in therapeutic antibody developments and identify structurally conserved immune hotspots linked with their possible inhibitory mechanism. Finally, we discuss progress in GN/GC-based vaccine research and clinical trials, highlighting key achievements and remaining challenges to overcome the public health threat posed by orthohantaviruses.
abstract.translated_zh: 正汉坦病毒是一类全球分布的啮齿动物传播的RNA病毒，常与人类严重且常致命的疾病相关。南美洲地方性流行的安第斯病毒已证实可发生人传人，凸显其大流行潜力，但目前尚无有效治疗方法，且仅有汉坦病毒和首尔病毒的疫苗。抗病毒干预的关键靶点是病毒表面展示的四聚体GN/GC刺突蛋白。本文综述了这些刺突蛋白的分子和细胞学新见解，重点介绍其在病毒感染周期中的作用，如受体介导的细胞进入、膜融合、病毒组装和细胞释放。我们还审视了治疗性抗体的最新进展，并识别了与其可能抑制机制相关的结构保守免疫热点。最后，讨论了基于GN/GC的疫苗研究和临床试验的进展，强调了关键成就及克服正汉坦病毒公共卫生威胁的剩余挑战。

sentences.text: Orthohantaviruses are globally distributed, rodent-borne RNA viruses associated with severe and often lethal disease in humans.

The documented person-to-person transmission of Andes virus, endemic in South America, underscores their pandemic potential, and yet no effective treatments are available, while vaccines exist only for Hantaan and Seoul viruses.
Key targets for antiviral intervention are the tetrameric GN/GC spikes, which are displayed on the viral surface.

Here we review current molecular and cellular insights into these spikes, focusing on their roles during the viral infection cycle, such as receptor-mediated cell entry, membrane fusion, viral assembly, and cell exit.
We also examine recent advances in therapeutic antibody developments and identify structurally conserved immune hotspots linked with their possible inhibitory mechanism.

Finally, we discuss progress in GN/GC-based vaccine research and clinical trials, highlighting key achievements and remaining challenges to overcome the public health threat posed by orthohantaviruses.

display_summary.zh: 正汉坦病毒是一类全球分布的啮齿动物传播RNA病毒，能引发严重致命疾病。安第斯病毒可人传，显示大流行风险。GN/GC刺突蛋白是抗病毒干预的关键靶点，涉及病毒进入、融合、组装和释放。最新研究聚焦其分子机制及治疗性抗体开发，并推进基于GN/GC的疫苗研究和临床试验。
display_summary.en: Orthohantaviruses are globally distributed rodent-borne RNA viruses causing severe and often fatal diseases. Andes virus can transmit person-to-person, indicating pandemic potential. The tetrameric GN/GC spikes on the viral surface are key antiviral targets involved in viral entry, fusion, assembly, and exit. Recent advances include molecular insights, therapeutic antibody development, and progress in GN/GC-based vaccine research and clinical trials.

ai_analysis.translated_abstract_zh: 正汉坦病毒是一类全球分布的啮齿动物传播的RNA病毒，常与人类严重且常致命的疾病相关。南美洲地方性流行的安第斯病毒已证实可发生人传人，凸显其大流行潜力，但目前尚无有效治疗方法，且仅有汉坦病毒和首尔病毒的疫苗。抗病毒干预的关键靶点是病毒表面展示的四聚体GN/GC刺突蛋白。本文综述了这些刺突蛋白的分子和细胞学新见解，重点介绍其在病毒感染周期中的作用，如受体介导的细胞进入、膜融合、病毒组装和细胞释放。我们还审视了治疗性抗体的最新进展，并识别了与其可能抑制机制相关的结构保守免疫热点。最后，讨论了基于GN/GC的疫苗研究和临床试验的进展，强调了关键成就及克服正汉坦病毒公共卫生威胁的剩余挑战。
ai_analysis.display_summary_zh: 正汉坦病毒是一类全球分布的啮齿动物传播RNA病毒，能引发严重致命疾病。安第斯病毒可人传，显示大流行风险。GN/GC刺突蛋白是抗病毒干预的关键靶点，涉及病毒进入、融合、组装和释放。最新研究聚焦其分子机制及治疗性抗体开发，并推进基于GN/GC的疫苗研究和临床试验。
ai_analysis.display_summary_en: Orthohantaviruses are globally distributed rodent-borne RNA viruses causing severe and often fatal diseases. Andes virus can transmit person-to-person, indicating pandemic potential. The tetrameric GN/GC spikes on the viral surface are key antiviral targets involved in viral entry, fusion, assembly, and exit. Recent advances include molecular insights, therapeutic antibody development, and progress in GN/GC-based vaccine research and clinical trials.
ai_analysis.one_sentence_takeaway: The GN/GC spikes of orthohantaviruses are critical molecular targets for understanding infection mechanisms and developing antiviral therapies and vaccines.
key_findings: Orthohantaviruses cause severe and often lethal diseases in humans (A1).
Andes virus can transmit person-to-person, highlighting pandemic potential (A2).
No effective treatments exist; vaccines are only available for Hantaan and Seoul viruses (A2).
The tetrameric GN/GC spikes on the viral surface are key targets for antiviral intervention (A3).
GN/GC spikes play roles in receptor-mediated cell entry, membrane fusion, viral assembly, and cell exit (A4).
Recent advances in therapeutic antibody development identify structurally conserved immune hotspots linked to inhibitory mechanisms (A5).
Progress in GN/GC-based vaccine research and clinical trials shows key achievements and remaining challenges (A6).
ai_analysis.significance: Understanding the molecular determinants of the GN/GC spikes is crucial for developing effective antiviral therapies and vaccines against orthohantaviruses, which pose a significant public health threat due to their severe disease outcomes and pandemic potential.
ai_analysis.limitations: The abstract does not specify study limitations; detailed limitations are unavailable due to lack of full text (A1-A6).
ai_analysis.evidence_strength: The evidence is based on a review article summarizing current molecular and clinical research findings; full text not available for detailed evaluation.
uncertainties: Specific molecular mechanisms and structural details of GN/GC spikes are not detailed in the abstract.
Efficacy and safety data of therapeutic antibodies and vaccines are not provided.
No information on clinical trial phases or outcomes is available.
